div.head: The British Prime Minister (Churchill) to President Roosevelt Copy of telegram obtained from the Franklin D. Roosevelt Library at Hyde Park, N.Y.
div.p: No. 796. Your number 628. I have given instructions to the Food Minister that no long-term contract is to be negotiated for the next couple of months or so and that we are to proceed meanwhile on a month to month basis during which time we can discuss the matters further.Text of the foregoing sentence amended as requested by Prime Minister Churchill in telegram 797, October 14, 1944, not printed.

We are worried for fear that French, Belgian and presently the Dutch will come into the market with plenty of gold. Nevertheless we shall make no arrangement at present time but later on I must address you again on the subject.
